headline: Agent orchestration consolidated this quarter as the Model Context Protocol and multi-agent tooling moved into production while standalone harness techniques weakened.
body: The quarter centered on agentic plumbing: Model Context Protocol and routing led the technique rotation, with WebMCP proposed as a web standard to expose typed tools to agents and tutorials grounding MCP client-server architecture against enterprise data in Looker, Snowflake, Linear, and Notion [2][8][9]. Multi-agent systems scaled in practice, with Cursor running 128 coding agents at once over inter-agent messaging and WorkOS dispatching agents to isolated git worktrees with verification gates and voice briefs [4][5][11]. Meanwhile single-session harness and recursive self-improvement weakened, the latter resurfacing mainly in agent-loop overviews touching memory systems and agentic payments [0]. New entrants clustered in narrow technical niches including text-to-CAD, faithful generation, phase diagram, and Q-target, none yet tied to the quarter's main agentic thrust.
